title: Windows Update Failed
cardText: Diagnose Windows updates that fail to download, install, or complete successfully.
Check internet connectivity, storage space, pending restarts, update services, system files, and update cache issues.
Separate simple update problems from operating system corruption or hardware faults.
text: This guide is for Windows updates that fail, become stuck, repeatedly retry, or generate error messages.
Start with basic checks such as internet access, free disk space, and pending restarts.
Then investigate update services, cache corruption, security software interference, and system file integrity.
Escalate persistent failures that survive repair attempts.

checkErrorMessage.title: Check for an update error message
body: Open Windows Update and note any error codes or messages.
Error codes can provide clues about the cause of the failure.
Record the code before making major changes.

checkInternetAccess.title: Check internet connectivity
body: Open a browser and test multiple websites.
Windows Update requires reliable internet access to download updates.
If websites fail to load, troubleshoot the network connection first.

useNoInternetGuide.title: Resolve internet connectivity first
body: Windows Update cannot function correctly without internet access.
Use the No Internet Connection guide to restore connectivity.
Retry updates after the internet connection is working.

checkPendingRestart.title: Check for a pending restart
body: Windows may require a restart before additional updates can install.
Look for Restart Required messages.
Restart the computer before continuing.

restartComputer.title: Restart the computer
body: Save your work and restart Windows.
After startup completes, run Windows Update again.
Many update failures are resolved by a restart.

checkDiskSpace.title: Check free disk space
body: Windows updates require free space for downloads, extraction, backups, and installation.
Low storage can cause updates to fail or become stuck.
Check available space on the system drive.

freeDiskSpace.title: Free disk space
body: Delete temporary files, empty the recycle bin, and remove unnecessary software if appropriate.
Use Storage Settings or Disk Cleanup to remove safe temporary data.
Retry Windows Update after freeing space.

runTroubleshooter.title: Run Windows Update Troubleshooter
body: Use the built-in Windows Update Troubleshooter.
It can automatically repair common update problems.
Apply any recommended fixes and test updates again.

checkUpdateType.title: Determine update scope
body: Check whether one update is failing or all updates are failing.
A single failing update often indicates a package-specific issue.
Multiple failures may indicate a broader Windows problem.

checkSpecificUpdate.title: Check specific update
body: Identify the update KB number if available.
Search for known issues affecting that update.
Some updates are temporarily withdrawn or known to fail on certain systems.

waitForReplacementUpdate.title: Known update issue
body: The failing update has a known issue.
Monitor Microsoft guidance and wait for a replacement or revised update.
Install other updates normally.

manualInstallUpdate.title: Attempt manual installation
body: Download the update manually from the Microsoft Update Catalog if appropriate.
Install it manually and monitor for errors.
This can bypass some Windows Update delivery issues.

singleUpdateResolved.title: Update installed successfully
body: The update installed manually.
The issue was likely related to Windows Update delivery rather than the update itself.
Continue installing remaining updates normally.

checkUpdateServices.title: Check Windows Update services
body: Verify that Windows Update related services are running.
Windows Update, Background Intelligent Transfer Service (BITS), and Cryptographic Services are commonly required.
Stopped services can prevent updates from downloading or installing.

restartUpdateServices.title: Restart update services
body: Restart the affected update services.
After the services restart, try Windows Update again.
Service failures can sometimes occur after crashes or incomplete updates.

checkSecuritySoftware.title: Check security software interference
body: Some antivirus or endpoint protection products can interfere with updates.
Review security software logs and update settings.
Temporarily testing without interference may help identify the cause.

securitySoftwareIssue.title: Security software issue found
body: The update failure appears related to security software.
Adjust configuration or update the security software.
Retest updates after the changes are made.

resetUpdateCache.title: Reset Windows Update cache
body: Corrupted update cache files can cause repeated failures.
Resetting the SoftwareDistribution and Catroot2 folders can force Windows to rebuild update data.
Retry updates after the cache is rebuilt.

checkSystemFiles.title: Check Windows system files
body: Corrupted operating system files can prevent updates from installing.
Run System File Checker (SFC) and Deployment Image Servicing and Management (DISM) repairs if appropriate.
These tools repair missing or damaged Windows components.

repairSystemFiles.title: Repair Windows system files
body: Allow SFC and DISM repairs to complete.
Restart the computer afterwards.
Test Windows Update again.

checkDateTime.title: Check system date and time
body: Incorrect system date, time, or time zone settings can cause update authentication failures.
Verify the clock is correct and synchronized.
Retry updates afterwards.

correctDateTime.title: Correct system date and time
body: Fix the system clock and synchronize it with an internet time source.
Retry Windows Update after correcting the settings.

checkDiskHealth.title: Check drive health
body: Storage problems can cause update failures and file corruption.
Check for SMART warnings, disk errors, unusual delays, or storage-related event log entries.
A failing drive should be treated as a data protection issue.

replaceFailingDrive.title: Drive failure suspected
body: Back up important data immediately.
Replace the failing drive and reinstall or restore Windows if necessary.
Update failures are often secondary symptoms of storage problems.

checkEventLogs.title: Review update-related event logs
body: Check Event Viewer for update, servicing, or installation errors.
Repeated errors can identify the component responsible for failures.
Document any recurring codes or messages.

researchErrorCode.title: Research update error
body: Use the recorded error code or event log message to identify the failing component.
Many update errors have documented Microsoft fixes.
Apply any relevant remediation steps.

applySpecificFix.title: Apply update-specific fix
body: Follow the documented fix for the identified error.
Retest updates after applying the repair.
Document the resolution for future reference.

repairInstallWindows.title: Advanced Windows repair required
body: Basic update troubleshooting did not resolve the problem.
Consider an in-place repair install of Windows, operating system recovery, or a clean reinstall.
Back up important data before major repair operations.
